factors.name: Fed Hawkish Surprise (Warsh FOMC)
factors.summary: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh delivered hawkish surprise mid-week, signaling rates held higher for longer and delaying potential cuts to December. This crushed gold (-1.69% Friday, -3 weeks losses) and supported USD (DXY rallying on rate-hike repricing). Long-duration growth equities typically compress on hawkish real-rate surprises.

factors.name: Real Yields (TIPS proxy)
factors.summary: US Treasury yields rose sharply post-FOMC as market repriced Fed hold-higher narrative. Real yields widening is bearish for long-duration Nasdaq names (mega-cap unprofitable growth, AI/cloud). Gold's 3-week loss confirms real-rate pressure.

factors.name: USD Strength Rally
factors.summary: USD/DXY rallying on Warsh hawkish repricing; USD/JPY at 1986 highs (161.30+), USD/CAD +1.34% for week. Strong dollar pressures Nasdaq earnings (heavy international revenue for AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL) and headwind for EM growth.

factors.name: Oil/Geopolitical Risk Repricing
factors.summary: US-Iran interim peace deal eased oil premium; Brent back to ~$80 from $85+ levels. Lower energy costs reduce inflation risk and ease Fed's upside surprise risk near-term. However, ceasefire fragile (Lebanon clashes, Iran delaying talks), so benefit may be transient.

factors.name: Tech Sector Sentiment & AI Capex Questions
factors.summary: News coverage highlights creeping questions on AI usefulness/spend ROI. Charles Schwab prediction market entry and Kalshi IPO chatter signal retail/institution hedging/speculating rather than conviction buying. Hyperscaler capex guidance crucial forward, not yet published post-FOMC.

factors.name: Mega-cap Earnings Calendar
factors.summary: No major mega-cap earnings mentioned in latest news window. Calendar currently clear; next driver will be earnings beats/misses and forward guidance on cloud capex (AWS, Azure, GCP) — likely weeks away.

factors.name: Market Momentum (4h/24h action)
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.05% (4h), +0.66% (24h) — minimal intraday/daily momentum. Thursday bounce after FOMC close (market records on options flow) suggests potential short squeeze or dealer rehedge, not conviction rally. Suggests late-cycle phase 2 positioning.

factors.name: VIX/Volatility Regime
factors.summary: No explicit VIX data in news, but options activity (Schwab, Kalshi, SATA/STRC leverage liquidations, $13B BTC expiry Friday) suggests elevated hedging/gamma management. Thin Juneteenth trading may amplify micro moves.

factors.name: Upcoming PCE & Flash PMI Data
factors.summary: Major US inflation (PCE) and global PMI data due next week. PCE surprise hot = re-confirm Warsh hawkish repricing (BEARISH equities, BULLISH USD). Cool surprise = relief rally. No data yet, risk is two-sided.

factors.name: Sector Rotation & Earnings Risk
factors.summary: Starbucks cutting UK/HK jobs; NetJets crash; Alamos Gold cut guidance on earthquake; Bain Capital CLO default (post-2008 first in Europe). Microeconomic data points suggest slower growth/earnings headwinds beneath surface rally.

screening.narrative: NAS100 caught between hawkish Fed repricing (Warsh FOMC mid-week crushed gold, inflated real yields, strengthened USD) and temporary geopolitical relief (US-Iran deal reduced oil premium). Thursday's post-close bounce to records appears driven by late-cycle options dynamics and dealer rehedging rather than conviction. Friday's flattish close (+0.66% in 24h) after holiday-thinned trade suggests momentum is stalling; market awaits next major catalyst (PCE inflation, flash PMIs, mega-cap earnings guidance) to confirm direction. Real-rate pressure remains the dominant headwind for long-duration growth names (core Nasdaq holdings).
catalysts_ahead: PCE inflation data (early next week) — hot print would extend Warsh hawkish repricing, cold print could spark relief rally
Global flash PMIs (next week) — Eurozone, US, China manufacturing/services; soft data confirms growth slowdown, hard data suggests inflation sticky
Mega-cap earnings guidance on cloud/AI capex (MSFT Azure, GOOGL GCP, AMZN AWS, META capex) — currently absent from recent news, critical forward signal
Fed Governor Waller speech (Monday) — early signal for further hawkish/dovish positioning
FOMC minutes (late June or early July) — will clarify Warsh's hawkish stance and rate-cut timing outlook
screening.risk_warning: If PCE comes in cool (deflationary surprise), or if major hyperscaler (MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN) posts strong earnings + bullish AI/capex guidance next week, market could snap higher, invalidating bearish real-yield thesis. Conversely, if geopolitical tension re-escalates (Iran/Lebanon), or if Friday's momentum evaporates on Monday reopening, could accelerate decline below 30200. Thin liquidity (Juneteenth) exacerbates whipsaw risk.
screening.entry_guidance: Move extended from Wednesday FOMC shock; late to short aggressively. If NAS100 rallies to 30500–30600 resistance early week, consider tactical short with tight stop above 30650. Alternatively, wait for PCE data (early week) before taking conviction directional stance; current NEUTRAL bias reflects conflicting headwinds and extended move.

factors.name: Fed Policy - Hawkish Surprise
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh's first FOMC decision (Wed) delivered hawkish messaging with repeated references to task forces delaying rate cuts; market repriced to higher terminal rates and delayed cuts to December or later. This compresses growth multiples.

factors.name: Real Yields & Duration Risk
factors.summary: 10Y Treasury yields rose sharply post-FOMC; gold fell 1.69% on Friday and extended a third consecutive weekly loss as real yields spiked. Long-duration Nasdaq names (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, TSLA) are sensitive to real rate widening.

factors.name: USD Strength
factors.summary: DXY rallied on Fed hawkish repricing; USD/JPY hit 161.30 (highest weekly close since 1986). Strong dollar headwind for mega-cap international revenue exposure (40%+ of NQ earnings).

factors.name: Geopolitical Relief Fade
factors.summary: US-Iran interim deal (early week) sparked risk-on rally and oil selloff, but relief has stalled; fighting resumed in Lebanon, talks postponed. Safe-haven unwind reversed course midweek once Fed tightness took center stage.

factors.name: Tech Earnings Cycle - TBD
factors.summary: No major mega-cap earnings reported in last 24h. Upcoming NVDA, MSFT, AAPL earnings dates remain key catalysts but not yet priced. AI capex narrative still intact but questioned (per Friday comments on AI spend utility).

factors.name: Market Structure & Sentiment
factors.summary: Despite S&P 500 rally on Thursday evening, NAS100 is up only +0.66% on 24h and +0.05% on 4h—lagging, not confirming momentum. Friday was holiday-thinned (Juneteenth), reducing conviction. Options positioning shows bears holding upper hand in upcoming expirations.

factors.name: QQQ/SMH Sector Momentum
factors.summary: Chip stocks rallied Friday despite hawkish Fed (counterintuitive), but overall breadth weak. No fresh semiconductor supply chain disruption, but TSMC/ASML sentiment muted on higher rates.

factors.name: Crypto Correlation (BTC Proxy)
factors.summary: Bitcoin miners struggling (JPMorgan: BTC ~19% below $78k production cost, 20% of industry unprofitable). BTC options show bearish bias; correlation to risk assets remains intact. NQ-BTC downside linkage a risk.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught in a Fed tightness rip: Warsh's Wednesday hawkish surprise triggered a Treasury sell-off and real-yield spike that crushed gold and growth multiples. The geopolitical relief from the US-Iran deal (which sparked early-week strength) has faded as fighting resumed and macro overshadows. Price action is muted (+0.66% 24h) with holiday-thinned Friday trade and no follow-through on Thursday's S&P bounce. Mega-cap tech earnings (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL) remain TBD; absent fresh AI capex surprises or Fed dovish reversal, upside is capped by real-rate headwinds.
catalysts_ahead: NVDA earnings (date TBD, high single-name gamma impact ±2-3% on NQ)
MSFT earnings (date TBD, cloud capex guidance critical)
AAPL earnings (date TBD)
PCE inflation data (next week ~likely, could trigger Fed re-pricing)
Fed Warsh commentary / task force updates
Ongoing US-Iran talks (risk sentiment barometer)
10Y Treasury yield moves (inverse proxy for tech multiple expansion)
screening.risk_warning: If Fed signals pivot toward rate cuts, or inflation surprises cool (PCE <2.5% YoY), real yields collapse and Nasdaq 100 could rip +2-3% on multiple re-expansion. Conversely, if earnings disappoint or AI capex guidance weakens, further downside is possible. Upcoming holiday season may amplify volatility. Markets into FOMC and earnings are choppy; early entry high-risk.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is PHASE_2 (Fed repricing in progress, real-yield shock felt but not yet absorbed). Wait for 4h pullback to 30000–30200 before considering longs; shorts should wait for resistance retest at 30600+ before entering. Avoid chasing ahead of PCE and earnings clarity.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Real Yields
factors.summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh signaled hawkish stance with task forces on rate decisions, implying rates held higher for longer. Gold dropped 1.69% Friday on Fed repricing; real yields remain elevated, compressing long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD/JPY hit 161.81 (two-year high), DXY rallying on hawkish Fed expectations. Strong dollar historically headwind for Nasdaq multinational revenue and relative valuation of growth equities.

factors.name: Geopolitical Relief (US-Iran Deal)
factors.summary: US-Iran interim peace agreement removed war premium from markets, oil rolled back from $80+ to $77 WTI. Risk-off hedges unwinding, supporting risk-on sentiment and equities broadly.

factors.name: Tech Earnings & Capex Momentum
factors.summary: Apple WWDC and pending mega-cap earnings not yet released; no new forward guidance on hyperscaler capex or AI demand in this news window. Awaiting Q2/Q3 commentary.

factors.name: AI & Semiconductor Sentiment
factors.summary: Questions emerging about AI spend sustainability and ROI (per investinglive commentary on 'usefulness'). Prediction market Kalshi IPO chatter suggests market saturation in some tech subsectors; no bullish catalyst in semiconductor supply news.

factors.name: Treasury Yields & Duration
factors.summary: 10-year yields remain elevated on Fed hawkishness; no dovish catalyst in sight. Long-duration growth stocks (NDX core holdings) face headwind as real rates stay sticky.

factors.name: Macro Uncertainty & Political Risk
factors.summary: UK PM Starmer considering resignation over weekend (political instability); Middle East ceasefire described as 'fragile' with already two killed post-deal. Volatility risk remains elevated.

factors.name: Price Action & Momentum
factors.summary: NAS100 up only +0.05% in 4h and +0.66% in 24h despite geopolitical relief and equity upside on Thursday. Minimal follow-through suggests exhaustion or consolidation.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq reversed hard after Fed Chair Warsh's hawkish press conference Wednesday, despite a US-Iran interim peace deal removing geopolitical premium. Long-duration growth multiples are under pressure from elevated real yields and strong dollar, which compound the headwind. Tech sentiment fragmented: AI capex enthusiasm cooling while mega-cap earnings remain ahead; no new catalysts confirmed in the news window. Price action shows only modest upside follow-through (+0.66% in 24h), hinting consolidation or retreat into Friday (Juneteenth, thin liquidity in US markets).

PCE (Core CPI equiv) data — next major inflation print, could reprices Fed expectations
Apple, Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Meta Q2 earnings & forward guidance (June–early July window)

screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Fed signals dovish pivot or delays rate hikes past Dec 2026; (2) new inflation data (CPI/PCE) comes in cool, forcing real yield collapse; (3) Middle East ceasefire holds and durably removes geopolitical premium, lifting risk appetite; (4) mega-cap earnings beat with upside guidance on AI/cloud capex; (5) USD weakens sharply on carry unwind. Conversely, ceasefire failure or new escalation, hawkish FOMC repricing, or real yields rising further would extend bearish pressure.
screening.entry_guidance: Move minimal (+0.66% in 24h), no directional confirmation yet. Stay sidelined or scale shorts only on retest of 30500 resistance; longs should wait for break-and-hold above 30700 with positive earnings catalysts. Avoid chasing into Friday thin liquidity (Juneteenth holiday). Watch PCE data and FOMC guidance next week as true catalysts.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Surprise (Warsh Press Conference)
factors.summary: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh signaled higher rates for longer and formed task forces on rate decisions, contrasting Trump's rate-cut expectations. This repriced real yields higher, pressuring long-duration growth/tech valuations.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury & Real Yields
factors.summary: US Dollar rallied on hawkish Fed repricing; gold sold off 1.69% on Friday as yields rose. This rising-rate environment directly compresses Nasdaq multiples, especially unprofitable AI/growth names.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal (Interim)
factors.summary: Initial relief rally Thursday (+0.8% Thursday in S&P 500) as oil fell from geopolitical premium; however, Lebanon clashes and delayed nuclear talks Friday signal fragility. War premium unwound but not confidence-building.

factors.summary: No major NAS100 component earnings in the immediate 24-72h window announced in news. AAPL WWDC passed (Q3/Q4 impact TBD). Lack of near-term catalyst removes upside tailwind.

factors.name: AI/Semiconductor Capex Debate
factors.summary: News mentions 'questions creeping in about AI usefulness and spend' but no specific guidance downgrades yet. Tesla, Nvidia, and cloud giants' capex plans remain uncertain heading into earnings cycles.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD Index rallying on Fed hawkish tilt; USD/JPY at highest weekly close since 1986. Strong dollar pressures international revenue of Nasdaq constituents (Apple, Microsoft, Google have ~50% non-US exposure).

factors.name: Technical: Weekly Momentum

factors.summary: No specific QQQ, XLK, or SMH flow data in news window. Holiday-thinned US trading (Juneteenth Friday) limits conviction on positioning changes.

factors.summary: Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) data expected next week; market repricing hawkish. Any hot print could extend real-rate selloff; cool print could stabilize valuations.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq opened Friday's holiday-thinned session flat (+0.66% on the day), caught between conflicting narratives: (1) hawkish Fed surprise from Chair Warsh on Wednesday, which repriced real yields sharply higher and triggered gold/commodities selloff; (2) fragile US-Iran peace deal relief that sputtered amid renewed Lebanon fighting and delayed nuclear talks. The initial Thursday equity rally (+0.8% S&P 500) proved unsustainable as traders refocused on tighter Fed policy and rising long-duration growth multiple compression. No major tech earnings catalyst imminent; markets are in a wait-and-see mode ahead of next week's PCE data.
catalysts_ahead: PCE (Personal Consumption Expenditures) release next week — any upside surprise re-validates hawkish Fed repricing, extends selloff
NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN earnings (dates TBD in coming weeks) — forward capex/AI demand guidance critical
US-Iran nuclear negotiations resume (timing unclear, Lebanon violence a wildcard)
Prediction market regulation updates (Charles Schwab S&P 500 options launch mentioned)
Further Fed speakers and commentary from Warsh (task force timeline clarity)
screening.risk_warning: If PCE prints cool (lower than 2.4% YoY expected) or Fed signals flexibility on the rate path, real yields could compress sharply, triggering a rebound in mega-cap growth. Conversely, if Iran deal collapses and oil spikes, geopolitical premium re-emerges, potentially lifting commodities and energy at tech's expense. Thin holiday trading may exaggerate moves in either direction; confidence should be low until volume normalizes.
screening.entry_guidance: Move extended into Friday close with minimal 4h follow-through (+0.05%). Do not chase shorts or longs. Wait for retest of 30500–30600 resistance (with volume) before considering fresh entries, or watch for a pullback to 30100–30200 support for long-side reversal trades once PCE clarity arrives.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Repricing
factors.summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh signaled rates staying higher for longer during first FOMC presser (Wed 2026-06-20). Market repriced for delayed rate cuts; 10Y yields rose, real yields firmed. This compressed long-duration growth multiple valuations, which is NAS100-negative.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal (Geopolitical Relief)
factors.summary: US-Iran ceasefire deal and Strait of Hormuz reopening reduced oil premium (Brent fell from ~$85 to $80, WTI near $77). Lower energy costs benefit high-capex tech sector; risk-off unwind supports equities. However, ceasefire fragility (reported drone strike in Lebanon post-agreement) limits conviction.

factors.summary: Hyperscalers (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA) are depleting cash reserves and raising debt for AI data center buildouts amid higher yields. Real yield (~2.2% implied from DXY strength + 10Y ~4.2%) hammers NPV of future hyperscaler cash flows; NAS100-heavy mega-cap valuations at risk.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Fed Rate Expectations
factors.summary: USD Index rallied on Fed's hawkish tilt; DXY near 52-week highs. Strong dollar pressures NAS100 constituent international revenues (estimated 40-50% of mega-cap earnings). Additionally, elevated real yields from USD-driven bond repricing weigh on growth equity duration.

factors.name: AI Spending Questions & NVDA Weakness
factors.summary: Several analysts/investors questioning AI spending usefulness and ROI (noted in news: 'Questions creeping in about AI usefulness and spend'). NVDA, TSLA, and semiconductor ETFs (SMH, SOXX) showed mixed tone late week despite Friday rally. No new hyperscaler capex guidance to reignite narrative.

factors.name: Positive Week Momentum & Risk-On Unwind
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.66% in 24h and +0.05% in 4h suggests modest consolidation after Thursday's strong rally (post-FOMC press conference close, when stocks rebounded). SpaceX IPO +37% in first week shows pent-up demand for high-growth/tech names; suggests some risk-on appetite remains.

factors.name: Inflation Data & PCE Watch
factors.summary: No major inflation surprises in the 24h news window. PCE (Personal Consumption Expenditures) release looming for next week; if hot, Fed repricing accelerates (BEARISH); if cool, growth multiple relief (BULLISH). This is a key upcoming catalyst.

factors.name: Earnings Blackout & Single-Name Catalysts
factors.summary: No mega-cap earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, NVDA, TSLA) announced in the past 24h. Conference season not imminent. Single-name risk is muted until next earnings cycle or guidance updates.

factors.summary: Strategy's preferred-stock (STRC) collapsed; reported liquidations of leveraged Bitcoin/crypto positions. While not directly NAS100, it signals financial stress, potential deleveraging, and risk-off sentiment among quant/systematic traders. Margin squeeze could spill into tech names.

factors.name: Consensus Bias History: 3 Consecutive NEUTRAL Reads
factors.summary: Your prior three haiku reads (2026-06-20 05:12, 08:53, 11:17) all scored NEUTRAL (4-5/10) with no confirmation triggered. This suggests chop, rangebound structure, and lack of decisive directional catalyst — consistent with current +0.66% / +0.05% modest moves.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught between two conflicting macro forces: Warsh's hawkish Fed (higher for longer, real yields grinding higher) vs. geopolitical relief (Iran ceasefire, lower oil risk premium). Thursday's post-FOMC rally (+1.2-1.5% spike) has already begun to fade; Friday consolidation (only +0.66% in 24h) and analyst chatter about AI spending ROI suggest exhaustion. Upcoming PCE inflation data and hyperscaler Q2 earnings will be the true north star; until then, expect chop around 30,300-30,600.

Until a clear directional catalyst (earnings, inflation, Fed pivot), treat as NEUTRAL rangebound chop.
screening.entry_guidance: Await retest of 30500-30600 (resistance) for potential short entry into real-yield headwinds; or wait for break of 30150 support for long entry on capitulation. Current consolidation is low-conviction; avoid chasing until PCE data or mega-cap earnings clarify direction.

Key headwinds: rising yields compress long-duration growth multiples, AI capex scrutiny is mounting, and crypto weakness signals risk-off undertones. The geopolitical relief (Hormuz closure claims aside, oil stable at $80) has largely been priced in. Ahead: PCE inflation data, FOMC minutes, and mega-cap earnings will determine whether the Fed's hawkish stance is validated or if growth fears re-emerge.

Conversely, if PCE cools materially, Fed pivot would be bullish for NDX and support higher valuations.
screening.entry_guidance: Market is choppy and consolidating; wait for PCE data (next week) before committing directional conviction. If bias shifts to bullish (cool inflation surprise + Fed dovish tilt), buy dips to 30200–30350. If hawkish (hot inflation + Fed hold), expect retest of 30000–30150. For now, stay neutral and avoid chasing; no high-conviction setup present.

factors.name: US-Iran ceasefire & Middle East de-escalation
factors.summary: US-Iran MOU signed, Strait of Hormuz reopening narrative bullish for growth equities and risk appetite. However, Iran now claims ceasefire violations and has closed Hormuz again (per multiple reports 13:30-14:54 UTC), creating renewed geopolitical uncertainty that caps upside.

factors.name: Oil market repricing
factors.summary: Brent back near $80, WTI near $77 — almost entire war premium reversed. Lower energy costs could support corporate margins, but lack of follow-through suggests market already priced the ceasefire benefit; Iran's renewed Hormuz closure is already being digested.

factors.name: USD strength & Fed rate hike expectations
factors.summary: DXY rallying on prospect of Fed rate hike; USD/JPY at highest weekly close since 1986 per FXStreet (19:27 UTC). Stronger USD headwind for US multinational tech earnings (NAS100 ~40% foreign revenue) and pressures long-duration growth multiples.

factors.name: Real yields and long-duration sensitivity
factors.summary: Fed hawkish tilt under Chair Kevin Warsh alongside rising Treasury yields; silver broke below 200-day SMA on USD strength and higher yields (20:12 UTC). Tech mega-caps extremely exposed to real yield moves; current setup signals tightening bias, unfavorable for high-multiple growth names.

factors.name: AI capex cycle and tech earnings
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO surged 37% in first week, sparking anticipation for OpenAI and Anthropic IPOs; strong appetite for AI infrastructure. However, no fresh earnings surprises or hyperscaler capex guidance in today's news flow; narrative is forward-looking but lacking confirmation catalysts.

factors.name: Crypto correlation and risk sentiment
factors.summary: Bitcoin battles $65K resistance; altseason narrative has 'disappeared' per Cointelegraph (19:09 UTC) as BTC dominance holds. No major volatility spike or risk-off flush; crypto weakness is measured, not panic-driven; NQ-BTC linkage shows cautious tone but no crash signal.

factors.name: Price action momentum
factors.summary: 4h change +0.05%, 24h change +0.66% — minimal intraday movement, flat-to-slightly-up bias. Bias history shows three consecutive NEUTRAL4/10 reads (13:59, 15:49, 17:25 UTC); no new confirmation or trigger has materialized.

factors.name: Macro data calendar and near-term catalysts
factors.summary: Fed policy decision expected next week under Warsh; PCE inflation data and global PMI releases looming. Absence of fresh CPI/PCE/NFP prints in today's window means no immediate data shock to reprrice NDX; market in holding pattern ahead of data.

factors.name: Mega-cap earnings and forward guidance
factors.summary: No AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, NVDA, or TSLA earnings or guidance announcements in today's news window. Lack of single-name catalysts removes 2-3% potential move drivers.

factors.name: Antitrust and regulatory headwinds
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports SEC commissioners gutting its leverage and DOJ refusing to put anti-weaponization fund in writing (22:53 UTC 6/19). Regulatory uncertainty for big tech remains elevated; no clear policy resolution in sight.

screening.narrative: NAS100 remains in a holding pattern despite geopolitical relief (US-Iran ceasefire) that briefly supported risk-on sentiment. The ceasefire narrative has already been partially priced in, and Iran's renewed Hormuz closure claim is reasserting geopolitical tail risks. Simultaneously, Fed hawkish bias under Warsh and rising Treasury yields are creating headwinds for long-duration tech multiples; USD strength adds pressure on multinational earnings. Tech mega-caps lack fresh earnings catalysts or hyperscaler capex confirmations, leaving the market without near-term directional conviction. Bias history shows three consecutive NEUTRAL reads, and current price action (flat-to-slightly-up over 4h/24h) reflects cautious indecision.
catalysts_ahead: Fed policy decision & Kevin Warsh guidance (next week)
US PCE inflation data (likely next week)
Global PMI releases
Mega-cap tech earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, NVDA, TSLA — dates TBD)
Iran-US diplomatic talks in Switzerland (ongoing)
Strait of Hormuz transit updates and oil market repricing
screening.risk_warning: A surprise dovish Fed pivot or cooler-than-expected PCE/CPI could flip bias to BULLISH and extend rallies beyond 30600. Conversely, escalation of Iran-Israel tensions or renewed Strait of Hormuz closure could trigger risk-off flush and break support at 30200. Lack of confirmed data or earnings catalysts means conviction is low; avoid chasing at current levels.
screening.entry_guidance: Market is in indecision mode (NEUTRAL bias, confidence 4). Avoid entries at current price; wait for either break of 30600 resistance on volume + Fed dovish signal, or pullback to 30200 support on a geopolitical flare-up before committing directional conviction.
